# Claude Code prompt — [5A] Tendering (PR B) — card-header summaries + override highlight + proportional cost appropriation

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background

Three Scope-of-Works / Quote enhancements that all hinge on the same
"auto-calculated but user-overridable, with a visual cue for overrides"
mechanic. Built as one cohesive PR so the shared mechanic only ships once.

## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `feat/tendering-override-wave`.
- **Migration naming**: use full `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*` timestamps for ALL
  migrations in this PR. Run `pnpm --filter @project-ops/api prisma migrate
  dev --name <slug>` to auto-generate the timestamp (do not hand-name folders
  with bare `YYYYMMDD_*` — those sort before timestamped folders for the same
  day and break ordering). This PR has TWO migrations: the ScopeCard override
  fields and the cost-line appropriation fields. Ensure the second is
  generated AFTER the first so the timestamps sort correctly.

---

## Part 1 — Override-highlight shared mechanic (build this FIRST)

### Token

Add to `apps/web/src/styles/tokens.css`:

```css
--surface-override: #FDD387;
```

### Shared component

`apps/web/src/components/OverrideField.tsx` (or a `useOverrideHighlight` hook —
choose whichever fits the existing patterns better):

- Accepts `isOverridden: boolean` and `onRevert: () => void`.
- When `isOverridden`, wraps the child input with a `background:
  var(--surface-override)` tint AND renders a small ↺ icon button on hover that
  calls `onRevert`.
- Highlight + revert icon are **screen-only** — never serialised to PDF /
  Excel exports.

### Adoption in this PR

Apply the mechanic to **three surfaces**:

1. **Dimension override fields (SQM / M³ / Tonnes)** in `ScopeQuantitiesTable.tsx`.
   They already track `dirty[k]` per PR #229. Wire `isOverridden = dirty[k]`,
   `onRevert = () => { setDim(k, ""); setDirty(d => ({ ...d, [k]: false })); }`.
2. **New card-header summary fields** (Part 2). Same dirty-flag mechanism.
3. **T&C clauses on the Quote tab** — when a clause's text differs from the
   IS-standard template, treat as overridden. `onRevert` resets the clause to
   the standard.

## Part 2 — Card-header summaries

For each `ScopeCard` (DEM, CIV, ASB, Other, and any future discipline), compute
and display in the card header (next to the existing markup + discipline picker):

### Formulas

For each item in the card:

- `itemPersonDays = item.men × item.days`
- For each plant cluster on the item: `clusterPlantDays = cluster.qty × cluster.days`

Card-level rollups:

- **People — peak crew** = `max(item.men)` across the card.
- **People — total person-days** = `Σ itemPersonDays`. Show both: e.g. *"Peak: 5 · 38 person-days"*.
- **Plant — per equipment description** (NOT by column index): group all plant
  clusters on the card by `equipment.description`. For each group, peak qty =
  `max(cluster.qty)` and plant-days = `Σ clusterPlantDays`. Display as a list:
  *"Robot excavator ×2, Bobcat ×1"*.
- **Card duration** — `max( labourDuration, max(perEquipmentPlantDuration) )` where:
  - `labourDuration = Σ itemPersonDays / max(item.men)`
  - `perEquipmentPlantDuration_i = (Σ clusterPlantDays for equipment i) / max(cluster.qty for equipment i)`
  - Rounded to 1 decimal place.

### Editability

Each summary field uses the same dirty-flag override mechanism as the dimension
fields. Default = auto-derived. User types → overridden, highlighted via Part 1.
Clearing the field reverts. Pure display override — **no back-cascade** to
per-item `men`/`days`/plant qty.

### Schema

Add to `ScopeCard` in `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`:

```prisma
peakCrewOverride        Int?     @map("peak_crew_override")
totalPersonDaysOverride Decimal? @map("total_person_days_override") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
plantSummaryOverride    String?  @map("plant_summary_override") // JSON-encoded [{ name, peakQty }]
durationOverride        Decimal? @map("duration_override") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
```

Migration: auto-generated full timestamp (`pnpm prisma migrate dev --name scope_card_header_overrides`). Pure additive.

### API

Extend `GET/PATCH /tenders/:id/scope/cards/:cardId` to:
- Compute auto-derived summary values server-side and include them in the GET
  response payload.
- Accept the four override fields on PATCH.

### UI

In `ScopeQuantitiesTable.tsx` (or wherever the card header is rendered), the
header zone becomes:

```
[Demolition]   Peak crew: 5 · 38 person-days   Robot excavator ×2 · Bobcat ×1   Duration: 7.6 days     Markup: 30 %    Discipline: DEM ▾
```

Each of the four summary cells uses the override mechanic.

---

## Part 3 — Proportional cost-line appropriation

The Quote → Edit "Adjust quote by ±% or ±$" control currently changes the total
only. Make it distribute proportionally across cost lines.

### Schema

On the cost-line model (find it — likely `ClientQuoteCostLine` or
`QuoteCostLine`):

- Add `baseValue Decimal @db.Decimal(14, 2)`. Existing `amount` becomes the
  displayed / adjusted value; `baseValue` is the un-adjusted reference.
- Backfill: `UPDATE ... SET base_value = amount` for existing rows.
- Add `overrideAmount Decimal? @db.Decimal(14, 2)` — set when the user has
  manually overridden the line's distributed value.

On `ClientQuote`:

- Add `adjustmentPercent Decimal? @db.Decimal(5, 2)` and `adjustmentDollar Decimal? @db.Decimal(14, 2)`.
- Enforce in the service: only one of the two is set at a time (mutually
  exclusive); setting one clears the other.

Migration: auto-generated full timestamp (`pnpm prisma migrate dev --name quote_cost_appropriation`) — must sort AFTER the ScopeCard migration created above.

### Distribution logic (server-side at render / save time)

- `U` = sum of `baseValue` for cost lines where `overrideAmount IS NULL`.
- `O` = sum of `overrideAmount` for cost lines where `overrideAmount IS NOT NULL`.
- `B` = sum of `baseValue` for ALL cost lines (base + overridden).
- `A` (adjustment dollar amount) =
  - if `adjustmentPercent` set: `A = (adjustmentPercent / 100) × B`.
  - if `adjustmentDollar` set: `A = adjustmentDollar`.
- For each cost line:
  - If `overrideAmount IS NOT NULL`: `displayedAmount = overrideAmount`.
  - Else: `displayedAmount = baseValue + A × (baseValue / U)`, rounded to 2dp.

Edge cases:
- `U == 0` (all lines overridden): adjustment can't be applied; ignore it with a
  warning toast in the UI. Save the adjustment value but it has no display effect.
- Cost options + provisional sums are **excluded** from this distribution —
  they're priced separately. Adjustment touches base cost lines only.

### Replacement, no compounding

When the user enters a new adjustment, REPLACE the previous one (set
`adjustmentPercent` / `adjustmentDollar` directly — no accumulation history).
Clearing both fields = no adjustment, lines display `baseValue + 0`.

### UI on Quote → Edit

- The existing adjustment input gets a "Distribute across lines" toggle
  (default: on). Off = legacy total-only behavior.
- When on, each cost line displays its distributed value via the override
  mechanic — manual line overrides show as tinted with ↺ revert (= clear
  `overrideAmount`).
- Adjustment summary line below the cost table: *"Quote adjusted by +10 % — $10,000 distributed across 4 lines (1 line overridden)"*.

---

## Tests

- Override-highlight component: visible iff `isOverridden`; ↺ click invokes
  `onRevert`.
- Card-header summary math (unit): peak/total/duration computed correctly with
  mixed labour + plant inputs.
- Cost appropriation math (unit): distribution respects overrides; rounds to
  2dp; replacement (no compounding) on new adjustment; edge case U=0 handled.
- Integration: edit a quote with overrides + adjustment, verify the persisted
  displayedAmounts match the formula.
- §6 pre-PR CI checklist green.

- Title: `[5A] Tendering — card-header summaries + override highlight + proportional cost appropriation`

- **Do NOT auto-merge.** Owner verifies live: override a dimension on a scope
  item, override a card-header field, apply a quote adjustment, override a single
  cost line.

## Scope

Three parts above only. Do not touch density (PR A), the floating bulk editor
(PR C), or the unified comms panel (PR D).
